Hey, all right, welcome back. So hopefully, as a result of the work we just did in our last section, you sent some campaigns to your database and you got some money. That was the idea. And hopefully going even further, you're like, man, I can just keep doing this stuff consistently and I can automate it and I can get more money than I was before without really having to do much more work, which is always great. And it is in that vein of getting more money than you were before without having to do much more work that we're going to keep going this week. So the purpose of this week is to take all the traffic and leads you're getting now and to maximize the profitability of that, right? So let me show you something. If you look here on my screen, I've run a report here for an ad campaign, actually a bunch of different ad campaigns. These are all different ad campaigns right here. And this is for the month of July. I'm filming this for you on July 27th. And this is a specific campaign that is promoting a front end loss leader product that costs $4 and it's got some upsells and some order bumps in here. And you can see I've spent 10 grand and I brought in 20 grand. So I almost doubled my money in profit here. So my net profit is 9,500 bucks, which is great, right? Now what's really cool about this and ultimately where I want to help you go is what I'm doing here is I'm like targeting in most of these campaigns, like just giant audiences. Like notice our most profitable one here is I'm literally targeting every human on the planet. Actually that's not our most profitable one. Our most profitable one here is everybody in the United States between 35 and 55, that one yielded the highest return. But notice like how huge and like seemingly generic these audiences are, right? The reason I'm able to do this at such a good profit is because of what I'm about to share with you in this series of videos. Essentially every type of business has its own sales process, right? And a sales process is a fancy way of saying what you do to turn a stranger into a customer. And every sales process has its own unique steps and it also has areas where you can maximize it. So the reason the ads I just showed you or rather the campaigns I just showed you that are selling something for four bucks have been working well and they're so profitable even though the targeting is like completely ridiculous is because I'm doing a pretty good job of maximizing the sales process. So what we're gonna do this week is we're gonna go through all the major types of sales processes for every business category. Your sales process fits into these, you're probably doing most of what I'm showing you. And I'm gonna give you the blueprints and the playbooks for making these sales processes more profitable right now. And the reason we wanna do this is number one, it could very well lead to you making more money immediately which would be super cool. And number two, when we start doing ads it's going to increase the likelihood of the ads being profitable, all right? So without further ado, I wanna give you an overview now of all of the business types and all of the sales processes. One thing I wanna share with you though, before I do is there's bound to be a point when you're looking at all of this stuff especially with the process maps and everything where you're gonna be like, oh my God, dude this is way more complicated than I thought. In reality it isn't, all right? What it really is, okay, is all of these different sales processes are essentially the same couple of key ingredients just used over and over again in a different order. That's it. So once you really start looking at these, you're like, wait a minute, these aren't really that complicated or fancy, they're just doing the same thing but in a different order depending on what it is I'm trying to sell and what the price points are. So don't let the initial illusion of complexity fool you. In reality we're just doing the same things in a different order depending on what it is you're trying to sell, all right? Cool, let me take you to the computer and give you a big overview of how this stuff works. Let's go. We don't really have that many and I'm really generalizing here but this is where it's pretty safe to do so. We got like information businesses, courses and stuff like that, coaching, masterminds, et cetera. You have services like accountant, lawyers, so forth, hire ticket stuff, you've got E-com and then you got retail like brick and mortar, right? And by the way, brick and mortar can be service. So by retail, I mean, okay, I got a clothing store or something like that. And these are kind of all of our categories here and what's cool is each category really has its own set of sales processes that aren't necessarily unique to that category but are pretty much standard and very common to that category. And so that's really, really helpful because if you know what they are you can know how to make them awesome because usually your clients are only doing them like 20% as good as they could or as well as they could, whatever the correct grammar is there, give me a break, I'm from Macon, Georgia. Grammar ain't my strong point, all right? So let's talk about the information category. I chose this one because clearly we're all familiar with this, right? So this will be a business of selling books and courses, stuff like that. It's a business that's selling coaching although technically some of the sales processes work in the service world would be applied to coaching, masterminds, membership sites, stuff like that. That's the info category. The services category, that's consulting, folks like us, accountants, they fall in that services law. There's another one that falls into the services and agency falls into services and there's all kinds of stuff that would. And these guys, they have very, very typical and relatively predictable sales process. So any of these folks that you talk to, they're all gonna have pretty much the same sales process and the sales process usually works pretty good. And then we've got Ecom, right? And Ecom's like, you're selling vitamins and stuff online or makeup or skincare products or notebooks or basically anything on Shopify, Ecom. You're selling stuff. Typically it's a high volume of low transaction size tickets, you know? And so that's Ecom and Ecom's got its own little tweaks and stuff that you can do to make it work a whole lot better. So here's a couple of points, right? That you need to keep in mind. Each category typically uses a category specific sales process. The key word there is typically though but it's not always the case, right? So for example, if we wanted to have an agency, we could sell that with a webinar to application, to phone consult, but we could also, and that's the service category, but we could also do the same thing for an info business that was selling a slightly higher ticket information product that did not provide any service or any human interaction of any kind, right? So they're not always like, you can only use this process in this category, but generally speaking, you kind of got a couple of sales processes that are useful in these four different categories. And the big deal is nailing that process. That's where the money is for us, is understanding how to make the process better and understanding the numbers behind the process. And again, the sales process is literally is how they turn someone into a customer. And so I'm gonna walk you through like actual process maps during this week's content, but our job really is to fully understand the process they're using and figure out the single most impactful ways to make it better one step at a time.
